subject: math
title: Grade 3 Math — EOG Practice Test 4
instructions: Read each question carefully. Choose the best answer. For gridded questions, enter just the number.

questions.questionText: What multiplication fact does this array of dots show?
questions.explanation: There are 6 rows of 3 dots: 6 × 3 = 18.

questions.questionText: Which number is shown by these base-ten blocks?
questions.explanation: 3 hundreds, 0 tens, and 6 ones make 306.

questions.questionText: What is 83 rounded to the nearest ten?
questions.explanation: 83 is closer to 80 than to 90, so it rounds to 80.

questions.questionText: What fraction of the circle is shaded?
questions.explanation: 5 of the 6 equal parts are shaded, so 5/6.

questions.questionText: What time does the clock show?
questions.explanation: The hour hand is between 7 and 8 and the minute hand points to 9, which is 45 minutes, so 7:45.

questions.questionText: What is 256 + 137? Enter just the number.
What is 700 − 245?
What is 42 ÷ 7?
Which comparison is true?
questions.explanation: 3/4 has more shaded parts than 1/4, so 3/4 > 1/4.

questions.questionText: How many sides does this shape have?
questions.explanation: A hexagon has 6 sides.

questions.questionText: How many goals did Carlos score?
questions.explanation: Carlos's bar reaches 9 on the scale.

questions.questionText: Which fraction does the point on the number line show?
questions.explanation: The number line is split into 4 equal parts and the point is at the first mark, which is 1/4.

questions.questionText: What fraction of this bar is shaded?
questions.explanation: 3 of the 8 equal parts are shaded, so 3/8.

questions.questionText: There are 7 baskets of oranges. Each basket has 5 oranges. How many oranges are there in all?
questions.explanation: 7 × 5 = 35 oranges.

questions.questionText: What is 8 × 7? Enter just the number.
A coach divides 63 cones equally among 9 players. How many cones does each player get? Enter just the number.
questions.explanation: 63 ÷ 9 = 7 cones each.

questions.questionText: Which fraction is equivalent to 2/3?
questions.explanation: 4/6 covers the same amount as 2/3, so they are equivalent.

questions.questionText: Art class starts at the time shown and lasts 35 minutes. What time does it end?
questions.explanation: 1:20 plus 35 minutes is 1:55.

questions.questionText: A poster is 9 inches long and 6 inches wide. What is its area, in square inches? Enter just the number.
questions.explanation: Area = 9 × 6 = 54 square inches.

questions.questionText: Each star stands for 4 stickers earned. How many stickers did Grade 3 earn?
questions.explanation: 6 stars × 4 = 24 stickers.

questions.questionText: Nadia had 215 beads. She used 87 to make a necklace. How many beads does she have left? Enter just the number.
Which name best describes a four-sided shape with exactly one pair of parallel sides?
questions.explanation: A trapezoid is a quadrilateral with exactly one pair of parallel sides.

questions.questionText: Which comparison is true?
questions.explanation: With the same denominator, fewer shaded parts means less: 5/8 < 7/8.

questions.questionText: Which fraction does the point on the number line show?
questions.explanation: The number line is split into 3 equal parts and the point is at the second mark, which is 2/3.

questions.questionText: There are 10 marbles in a bag. 4 of them are blue. What fraction of the marbles are blue?
questions.explanation: 4 out of 10 marbles are blue, so 4/10.

questions.questionText: In the number 582, what is the value of the digit 8?
questions.explanation: The 8 is in the tens place, so its value is 80.

questions.questionText: In the counting pattern 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, ..., select all the numbers that belong in the pattern.
questions.explanation: The pattern is multiples of 5. 30 and 45 are multiples of 5; 32 and 48 are not.

questions.questionText: Which expression has the same value as 9 × 4?
questions.explanation: Multiplication can be done in any order, so 4 × 9 = 9 × 4 = 36.

questions.questionText: A teacher made 5 rows of chairs with 6 chairs in each row. Then 8 students sat down. How many empty chairs are left?
questions.explanation: 5 × 6 = 30 chairs; 30 − 8 = 22 empty chairs.

questions.questionText: Each book costs $8. Marcus buys 4 books and pays with a $50 bill. How much change does he get back?
A rectangle has an area of 42 square meters. If one side is 6 meters long, how long is the other side, in meters? Enter just the number.
questions.explanation: Area = length × width, so 42 ÷ 6 = 7 m.

questions.questionText: A sandwich is cut into 4 equal pieces. Jordan eats 1 piece and his sister eats 1 piece. What fraction of the sandwich is left?
questions.explanation: 2 pieces were eaten, so 4 − 2 = 2 pieces are left out of 4, which is 2/4.

questions.questionText: Soccer practice started at 3:40 and ended at 4:25. How long did practice last?
questions.explanation: From 3:40 to 4:00 is 20 minutes, and 4:00 to 4:25 is 25 minutes: 20 + 25 = 45 minutes.

questions.questionText: Which fraction is the greatest?
questions.explanation: When the top number is 1, the smaller the bottom number, the larger the fraction. 1/3 is the greatest because 3 is the smallest denominator.

questions.questionText: Select all the expressions that equal 36.
Select all the fractions that are equal to 1/2.
questions.explanation: 2/4, 3/6, and 4/8 each equal 1/2. 2/3 is greater than 1/2.

questions.questionText: Select all the numbers that round to 500 when rounded to the nearest hundred.
questions.explanation: 472 rounds to 500 and 538 rounds to 500. 449 rounds to 400 and 551 rounds to 600.

questions.questionText: Select all the shapes that are quadrilaterals (shapes with exactly 4 sides).
questions.explanation: A rhombus, rectangle, and square each have 4 sides. A pentagon has 5 sides.

questions.questionText: A baker made 6 trays of cookies with 8 cookies on each tray. She sold 17 cookies. How many cookies are left? Enter just the number.
questions.explanation: 6 × 8 = 48 cookies made; 48 − 17 = 31 left.

questions.questionText: What is the area, in square units, of this rectangle?
questions.explanation: Area = 7 × 4 = 28 square units.
